title: achillea alpina subsp. subcartilaginea
intro: Achillea alpina subsp. subcartilaginea, also known as Alpine Yarrow or Himalayan Yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Achillea alpina subsp. subcartilaginea include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to create topical applications, salves, and tinctures for therapeutic purposes.
